content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/knowledge_base/2509.13348v4.pdf", "name": "2509.13348v4.pdf", "extension": ".pdf", "mime_type": "application/pdf", "size_bytes": 6062812, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": "2025-10-01\n\nTowards an AI-Augmented Textbook\n\narXiv:2509.13348v4 [cs.CY] 30 Sep 2025\n\nLearnLM Team, Google\n\nTextbooks are a cornerstone of education, but they have a fundamental limitation: they are a one-sizefits-all medium. Any new material or alternative representation requires arduous human effort, so\nthat textbooks cannot be adapted in a scalable manner. We present an approach for transforming and\naugmenting textbooks using generative AI, adding layers of multiple representations and personalization\nwhile maintaining content integrity and quality. We refer to the system built with this approach as Learn\nYour Way. We report pedagogical evaluations of the different transformations and augmentations, and\npresent the results of a a randomized control trial, highlighting the advantages of learning with Learn\nYour Way over regular textbook usage.\n\nKeywords: Personalized learning, generative education, content transformations\n\n1. Introduction\nRecent advances in generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen-AI) have the potential to revolutionize\neducation, but this potential is yet to be realized in full. It requires a responsible, multidisciplinary\napproach to weave together learning scie"}]

thoughts.subject: Defining the Output Structure
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on structuring the JSON output. I'm ensuring that each object includes `rel_path`, `title`, and `content_kind` fields. I'm considering how best to derive `title` and `content_kind` from the provided input data, such as `name`, `extension`, and `sample_text`. Determining how `domain` will be populated is the next step.

thoughts.subject: Mapping Input to Fields
thoughts.description: I'm now deep in the process of mapping the input file data to the desired output fields. I've begun parsing the provided sample text, and identified keywords that will contribute to the `title`, `content_kind`, and `domain` fields. The example data is proving invaluable for this step. Determining how the `confidence` field will be calculated is next.

thoughts.subject: Categorizing the Metadata
thoughts.description: I'm presently building out the fields for the example document. I'm extracting key data points. The `title` field is derived directly from the sample text. I've classified `content_kind` as "research_paper." The `domain` now encompasses "ai," "education," and "research." I'm also working on `lifecycle` and `confidence`, refining the output object's completeness.

thoughts.subject: Populating All The Fields
thoughts.description: I've just finished the first full pass at populating all the fields. I used the sample text and other provided inputs to create each of the necessary data points, as well as refining the data. I've classified `content_kind` to "research_paper." I've populated `tags`, `summary`, `entities`, `relations`, `recommended_zone`, and `canonical_priority`. Next up is ensuring the data is in valid JSON form.
